summary.intent: Implement pass-7 fixes for a Rust carousel component, addressing 5 issues found by a Codex review: navigating swipes routing through Transitioning state, loop-aware index clamping, accessibility label for indicator tablist, zero-duration auto-settle for navigate_to/AutoPlayTick, and documenting the on_root_keydown adapter-gating contract.
summary.outcome: All 5 fixes implemented and tested: 137 carousel logic tests pass, clippy clean, snapshots regenerated, spec synced, and xci-fast green. Changes committed and pushed with re-labeling.

repo: The carousel uses a multi-pass Codex review workflow where each pass surfaces threads (issues) that are polled via background tasks before implementation begins.
TransitionPlan::then and then_send re-dispatch both work for chaining state transitions, confirmed before relying on them for the settle_if_instant helper.
The agnostic core has no DOM target access, so on_root_keydown target-gating (nested-widget exclusion) is necessarily the adapter's responsibility — this is a key architectural boundary.
Navigating swipes must route through the Transitioning state rather than jumping directly to Idle/AutoPlaying; the old behavior was a state machine shortcut that skipped the transition lifecycle.
Loop-aware index clamping uses max_start_index() rather than last_index() — these differ when looping is enabled and items fill more than one viewport.

code.finding: settle_if_instant helper added to auto-settle zero-duration transitions; called from both navigate_to and AutoPlayTick handlers.
max_start_index() helper added for loop-aware index clamping; replaces last_index() in init and SyncProps clamps.
PointerUp rewritten so a navigating swipe routes through Transitioning state instead of jumping directly to Idle/AutoPlaying.
indicators_label added to Messages struct and applied as aria-label on the indicator tablist in indicator_group_attrs.
Spec updated across §1.3, §1.5, §1.6, §3.2, §4.1 to reflect max_start_index, settle_if_instant, PointerUp rewrite, indicators_label, and on_root_keydown adapter-gating contract.

workflow: Three existing swipe tests had to be updated after the PointerUp rewrite because they asserted old direct-to-Idle/AutoPlaying behavior — behavior-changing fixes require updating tests that encode the old behavior.
Snapshot regeneration is needed after any attr change to indicator_group (e.g. adding aria-label); the workflow is: build → run tests → regenerate snapshots → verify diff.
Variable name collision with a function name ('service') causes a Rust compile error with shadowing; rename the local variable to avoid it.
The workflow is: implement → tests → snapshots → spec sync → full validate → xci-fast → commit/push/re-label/reply/resolve/re-trigger.

friction: Multiple sequential reads of the same file section were needed to locate the right edit points, suggesting the file is large and navigation is non-trivial.
A compile error from variable shadowing a function name ('service') required an extra fix-and-rebuild cycle.
Three existing tests encoded the old (incorrect) PointerUp behavior and had to be manually updated after the fix was applied.
open_items: S5 (on_root_keydown adapter-gating) is doc-only — no TDD test was added since it's a documentation/contract clarification rather than a behavioral change; adapter implementations still need to enforce the gating.
The pass-7 workflow implies further passes (pass-8+) may follow with additional Codex review threads to address.
